steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows "Brooks's poetic ___" and precedes a three-item list (compressed lines; syncopated rhythms; a keen attention). The correct punctuation must legally introduce that list as an elaboration of "poetic style."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'style, terse,': a comma after "style" cannot introduce a list — it would join items in a series, but "terse" is an adjective that must stay attached to the list, not dangle between two commas. ✗
  • Choice B — 'style. Terse,': a period creates two sentences. The second would begin "Terse, compressed lines..." but that fragment has no main verb and cannot stand alone. ✗
  • Choice C — 'style; terse,': a semicolon joins two independent clauses. What follows is not an independent clause — it is a descriptive list, so a semicolon is incorrect here. ✗
  • Choice D — 'style: terse,': a colon after a complete clause introduces an elaboration or list. "Three signature elements of Brooks's poetic style" is that complete clause, and the colon correctly signals that the list names those elements. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
